title: Kablikia truncata
taxonomy: Kablikia truncata is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of the Caucasus and is often found in alpine meadows and rocky areas. The classification of Kablikia truncata places it within the genus Kablikia, which is a group of flowering plants that are closely related to the genus Cassiope. Kablikia truncata is a key indicator species for the alpine ecosystems of the Caucasus, and its presence is often used to monitor the health of these environments. The classification of Kablikia truncata is further supported by its unique combination of morphological and molecular characteristics.
